## Before you submit

> **New feature?** Please open an issue first and get approval from maintainers before writing code.
> PRs that introduce features without prior discussion may be closed.
>
> **Used AI assistance?** That is allowed, but review every line before submission.
> PRs that show no sign of self-review may be closed without detailed feedback.
>
> **Keep it focused.** One logical change per PR. If it is not ready, open as draft.

---

## What does this PR do?

<!--
A short summary of the change and why it is needed.
Link at least one issue using a GitHub closing keyword, for example:
- Closes #123
- Fixes: #123
- Resolved owner/repo#123
-->

Closes #

## How did you test this?

<!--
Tell us what you actually did to verify this works.
Include KOReader version/platform and key steps.
-->

## Screenshots or recordings

<!--
If this changes UI behavior, include before/after screenshots or a short recording.
-->

## Anything non-obvious in the diff?

<!--
Call out tricky decisions, trade-offs, or areas where you want reviewer focus.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] I've read through my own diff
- [ ] This PR was discussed in an issue and has maintainer approval (for new features)
- [ ] Tested in KOReader on at least one platform
- [ ] Network failure paths were considered (timeout/offline/server error), where applicable
- [ ] No credentials, tokens, or private endpoints were added to committed files
- [ ] No unintended files included (build artifacts, personal configs, local IDE noise)
